name: Mike Chen

past_mistakes.trade: Bought 15 shares of GME at $210 during the Reddit short squeeze frenzy
past_mistakes.lesson: FOMO trading is gambling, not investing. Reddit hype is not a thesis. Never again touching meme stocks.

past_mistakes.trade: Held TQQQ from $60 down to $22, panic sold at bottom for -38% realized loss ($3,200)
past_mistakes.lesson: Leveraged ETFs decay in volatile markets. Panic selling locks in losses. Should have never held TQQQ long-term.

investment_philosophy: Buy broad market index funds every month, ignore the noise, and let compounding do the heavy lifting over decades.
writing_voice: Straightforward, mildly self-deprecating American engineer. Uses real numbers, admits past mistakes openly, no financial jargon without explanation.
favorite_tools: Google Sheets for net worth tracking
current_year_goal: Max out 401k contribution ($23,500 limit in 2026) and hit $100k total invested portfolio by December.
